sections.paragraph: ἅπ. λεγόμ. Jud. 3:22 , according to Targ. Vulg., Luth. dung (comp. פֶּרֶשׁ) וַיֵצֵא הַפַּרְשְׁדֹנָה “and there came out dung” from the wound; but the ה paragogic rather requires it to be understood of a place at which anything came out. I prefer, therefore, “and (the sword) came out between his legs;” see the root, and פַּרְשֵׁז . In the gender of the verb וַיֵעֵא there is no difficulty, as the verb is rather far from its noun (compare Heb. Gramm. § 144, note 1, especially the instance, Zec 13:7).-LXX. Vatic. καὶ ἐξῆλθεν (Ἀὼδ) τὴν προστάδα, as if הַפַּרְשְׁדֹנָה were the same as הַמִּסְדְּרֹונָה verse 23 Jdg 3:23, but nothing can be imagined more frigid than such a repetition, and it is clear that verse 23 Jdg 3:23 passes on to something else. In like manner Ewald (Heb. Gramm. p. 519), interprets, he (Ehud) went out abroad (er ging ins Freie), comparing فرشد (see above, which does not avail much in this place), and פְּרָזֹו country.
